# Stack Dependencies - Nested Same-Name Example

Based on [RFC #5663](https://github.com/gruntwork-io/terragrunt/issues/5663) - Stack Dependencies via Autoinclude.

## What this demonstrates

The literal question from the RFC sketch:

```
config_path = stack.foo.path vs stack.foo.foo.path ?
```

This example sets up the exact case where a `stack` and a `unit` inside it share the same name (`foo`), so both forms are syntactically valid and refer to **different** things:

- `stack.foo.path` - the stack root, e.g. `.terragrunt-stack/foo`
- `stack.foo.foo.path` - the `unit "foo"` inside `stack "foo"`, e.g. `.terragrunt-stack/foo/.terragrunt-stack/foo`

Use `stack.<stack_name>.path` to depend on the **entire stack** (waits for all its units, exposes aggregated outputs as `dependency.<dep_name>.outputs.<unit_name>.<key>`). Use `stack.<stack_name>.<unit_name>.path` to depend on **one specific unit** within that stack.

## Directory structure

```
nested-same-name/
  catalog/
    stacks/
      foo/
        terragrunt.stack.hcl   # nested stack: defines unit "foo"
    units/
      foo/                     # produces output "val"
      bar/                     # consumes stack.foo.foo
  live/
    terragrunt.stack.hcl       # stack "foo" + unit "bar"
```

## Stack files

`catalog/stacks/foo/terragrunt.stack.hcl`:

```hcl
unit "foo" {
  source = "../../units/foo"
  path   = "foo"
}
```

`live/terragrunt.stack.hcl`:

```hcl
stack "foo" {
  source = "../catalog/stacks/foo"
  path   = "foo"
}

unit "bar" {
  source = "../catalog/units/bar"
  path   = "bar"

  autoinclude {
    dependency "foo_unit" {
      config_path = stack.foo.foo.path   # the unit "foo" inside stack "foo"

      mock_outputs_allowed_terraform_commands = ["validate", "plan"]
      mock_outputs = {
        val = "fake-val"
      }
    }

    inputs = {
      val = dependency.foo_unit.outputs.val
    }
  }
}
```

## How `stack.foo.foo.path` is built

`internal/hclparse/stack.go` defines `BuildComponentRefMap`: for each stack block it parses the child `terragrunt.stack.hcl` (via `DiscoverStackChildUnits`) and exposes every child unit as a nested attribute on the stack object:

```
stack = {
  foo = {
    path = "<abs path to stack root>"
    name = "foo"
    foo  = { path = "<abs path to unit foo>", name = "foo" }   # child unit
  }
}
```

That is why `stack.foo.foo.path` is reachable for `dependency.config_path` resolution.

## How to run

```bash
cd live
terragrunt stack generate --experiment stack-dependencies
terragrunt run --all --experiment stack-dependencies -- apply -auto-approve
```

Expected generated layout:

```
live/.terragrunt-stack/
  foo/
    .terragrunt-stack/
      foo/                # the inner unit "foo"
        main.tf
        terragrunt.hcl
  bar/
    main.tf
    terragrunt.hcl
    terragrunt.autoinclude.hcl
```

Expected `terragrunt.autoinclude.hcl` for `bar` (relative path to the inner unit):

```hcl
# Generated by Terragrunt. Do not edit manually.
dependency "foo_unit" {
  config_path                             = "../foo/.terragrunt-stack/foo"
  mock_outputs_allowed_terraform_commands = ["validate", "plan"]
  mock_outputs = {
    val = "fake-val"
  }
}

inputs = {
  val = dependency.foo_unit.outputs.val
}
```

Validate:

```bash
cat .terragrunt-stack/bar/.terragrunt-cache/*/input.txt
# Expected: Received: from-stack-foo-unit-foo
```

## Compare with depending on the stack root

If `config_path = stack.foo.path` were used instead, `bar` would block on the **whole** `stack.foo` and outputs would be accessed as `dependency.foo_unit.outputs.foo.val` (aggregated map keyed by unit name). See the prototype docs for "Dependencies on entire stacks".

## Clean up

```bash
terragrunt stack clean
```

## RFC reference

https://github.com/gruntwork-io/terragrunt/issues/5663
